title: Adolescent Well-Care Visit: Confidential Screening and a Positive Depression Screen
subtitle: A 15-year-old comes in for an annual check-up and soccer sports physical. A confidential HEADSSS interview reveals a positive PHQ-9 with passive suicidal thoughts. The nurse must screen, plan for safety, restrict lethal means, and connect her to care.

patient.name: A. Tanaka
patient.admitDate: Outpatient clinic. Annual well-care visit plus soccer sports physical, here with her mother. Tired and quieter lately with falling grades; confidential interview reveals depressive symptoms and passive suicidal ideation.

labs.v: Broken for danger, abuse, or court order.
Teens 12+ may consent to MH/repro/STI/substance care (CA).
Lock or remove firearms and medications.
Warning signs, coping, supports, crisis lines.
HPV/Tdap done; MenACWY booster at 16; flu.

labs.r: Gentle activity if MH team clears.

nursesNote: A. Tanaka, 15, presents for an annual well-care visit and soccer sports physical with her mother, who notes she is tired, quieter, and has falling grades. After a brief joint portion, the mother steps out for a confidential HEADSSS interview; confidentiality and its limits (imminent danger to self or others, abuse/neglect, court order) are explained first, and California minor-consent rules let a teen 12 or older consent to mental health, reproductive, STI, and substance-use care. Screening shows a PHQ-9 of 16/27 (moderately severe) with a positive item 9; a follow-up suicide screen (ASQ/C-SSRS) reveals passive ideation ('wishes she didn't exist') without plan, intent, or prior attempt — moderate risk. The nurse builds a Stanley-Brown safety plan, counsels lethal-means restriction (relocating the home firearms and securing medications), and arranges a same-day mental health intake with 988 and crisis-text resources. With the patient's consent, the mother is told the safety concerns and plan (not the full interview); immunization catch-up is addressed (HPV/Tdap complete, MenACWY booster due at 16, influenza given), and sports clearance is held pending mental-health stability.
providerOrders: Conduct a confidential HEADSSS interview; explain confidentiality and its limits
Administer the PHQ-9; for any positive item 9, perform a suicide-risk screen (ASQ/C-SSRS)
Build a safety plan and counsel lethal-means restriction (secure firearms and medications)
Arrange same-day mental health intake; provide 988 and crisis-line resources
Address ACIP immunization catch-up; hold sports clearance pending mental-health stability

opts.text: Confidentiality and its limits should be explained before the confidential interview
A PHQ-9 of 16/27 indicates moderately severe depression needing follow-up
A positive item 9 (thoughts of being better off dead) requires a suicide-risk assessment
Passive ideation without plan or intent still warrants a safety plan and means restriction
A normal physical exam means no mental health screening is needed
In many states a teen 12 or older can consent to mental health care
The confidential interview is conducted with the caregiver out of the room
Securing firearms and medications is a key suicide-prevention step
With the patient's consent, share the safety concerns and plan with the parent
Confidentiality is broken for imminent danger, abuse/neglect, or a court order
A competent teen's full interview details must always be shared with parents
A same-day mental health intake is better than sending her home unassessed

steps.title: Prioritize Hypotheses
steps.sub: Order the response to the positive screen

opts.text: Complete a suicide-risk assessment (ASQ/C-SSRS)
Ensure immediate safety and counsel lethal-means restriction
Create a safety plan with the patient
Arrange a same-day mental health intake
Engage the parent (with consent) and set follow-up
Explain confidentiality and its limits before the interview
Assess suicide risk after a positive PHQ-9 item 9
Counsel securing firearms and medications at home
Arrange a same-day mental health intake
Give the parent the full confidential interview without consent
Send the teen home with no risk assessment
Tell the teen nothing she shares is private
Hold sports clearance until mental health is stable

steps.title: Take Action
steps.inst: Rank the order of actions. #1 = FIRST.

opts.text: Conduct the confidential HEADSSS interview and screening
Perform the suicide-risk assessment and ensure safety
Build the safety plan and counsel means restriction
Connect to same-day mental health intake and crisis resources
With consent, brief the parent and arrange follow-up and immunizations
Passive ideation without plan or intent is managed with a safety plan and same-day intake
Firearms are relocated and medications secured at home
The parent is told the safety concerns and plan with the teen's consent
Influenza vaccine is given and the MenACWY booster is scheduled for age 16
The teen is cleared for competitive soccer immediately despite active symptoms
The full confidential interview is shared with the parent without consent
The positive item 9 is ignored because the total 'looked okay'
The teen is sent home with no mental health follow-up

reference: Aligned to the NCSBN NCLEX Test Plan client-need category: Health Promotion and Maintenance, using the NCSBN Clinical Judgment Measurement Model. Written from standard nursing references. Pending faculty review.
